ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for tiger prawn dumpling with mayonnaise; broader dumpling food product term used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Crustaceans, cooked — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for a prawn-filled dumpling dish; flaky pastry wrapper and mayonnaise components are not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for tiger prawn dumpling with mayonnaise. A0CKM (cooked crustaceans) is used as the primary ingredient anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite seafood dumpling dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 pieces of tiger prawn dumpling, approximately 90 g total, as typically served in Singaporean dim sum or hawker settings.

nutrient_source.title: Tiger Prawn Dumpling W/ Mayonnaise — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Tiger Prawn Dumpling W/ Mayonnaise (cr_id G00010691). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB entry and are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for prawn-filled pastry products; these fields are documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on wheat-flour dumpling pastry with protein-rich filling
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tiger prawn dumpling with mayonnaise. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat-flour-based dim sum dumplings with protein-rich fillings. The high protein content (8.71 g/100g) and low carbohydrate load (8.03 g/100g) moderate the glycemic response. Glycemic load per 90 g serving = (55 × 8.03 × 0.90) / 100 ≈ 4.0 g, classified as low GL. GI 55 is considered low according to the University of Sydney GI Database classification (low < 55, medium 56-69, high > 70).

hypertension.guidance: At 46 mg sodium per 100 g, this dish is suitable for individuals managing hypertension; however, patients should avoid additional high-sodium dipping sauces or extra mayonnaise, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.03 g/100g) with high protein moderating glycemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content (8.03 g/100g) and high protein (8.71 g/100g) slow gastric emptying and moderate postprandial glucose rise; a standard 3-piece serving (90 g) delivers approximately 7.2 g of carbohydrate and an estimated glycemic load of 4, making this a low-GL choice suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without additional sugary sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and low sodium; mayonnaise contributes omega-6 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is low in saturated fat (~0.8 g/100g) and sodium (46 mg/100g), making it broadly compatible with a heart-healthy diet; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to control total fat intake from mayonnaise and choose preparations that avoid trans fats in the pastry or dressing.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Tiger prawn provides lean protein and omega-3 fatty acids; low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tiger prawns supply lean protein and modest omega-3 fatty acids that support joint health; commercial mayonnaise may contain omega-6-rich vegetable oils which can be mildly pro-inflammatory in excess, so patients with rheumatoid arthritis should prefer olive-oil-based or reduced-quantity mayonnaise and verify the product is free of trans fats.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tiger Prawn Dumpling with Mayonnaise is a Singaporean Chinese-style dim sum item consisting of whole or chopped tiger prawns encased in a light, flaky pastry shell and finished with a dollop of special mayonnaise. Each serving typically comprises three pieces totalling approximately 90 g. The pastry exterior is crisp and golden when baked or fried, while the prawn filling is tender, juicy, and mildly sweet with a clean seafood flavour. The mayonnaise adds a creamy, slightly tangy richness that complements the natural sweetness of the tiger prawn. Nutritionally, the dish is relatively low in calories at 101 kcal per 100 g, with a high protein content of 8.71 g and a moderate fat level of 3.78 g, primarily from the mayonnaise and pastry. Carbohydrate content is low at 8.03 g per 100 g with negligible sugar (0.21 g), making the glycemic load per serving approximately 4 — a low-GL food. Sodium is very low at 46 mg per 100 g, which is favourable for individuals managing hypertension. Iron content is notable at 1.79 mg per 100 g, contributed by the prawn. The dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed in standard portions and without additional high-sodium condiments. It is commonly served at Singaporean dim sum restaurants, hawker centres, and Chinese-style buffets as a starter or side dish at lunch or dinner.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010691, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous prawn pastry entries and are clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, which is the approved GI source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific tiger prawn dumpling with mayonnaise preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat (0.8 g/100g) and dietary fibre (0.5 g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for shrimp-filled pastry products (FDC ID analogous reference) and are marked as adapted. Halal status is listed as false because commercial mayonnaise may contain non-halal ingredients; consumers should verify the specific product. The preparation method listed includes deep-fried, baked, and steamed as the dish may be prepared by any of these methods depending on the vendor; the HPB entry does not specify a single method.
